summary: In primary health centers where focus lies more broadly than deep specialization into particular fields such as psychiatry, there may lack dedicated full-time presence of psychiatrists who are typically found working within higher-tiered hospital structures or other specialist environments designed specifically with intensive psychological support services in mind.; Psychiatrist roles extend beyond just being present everywhere but rather concentrated based upon need assessment which often prioritizes tertiary level care over initial screening conducted by general practitioners even though some form of consultation can still occur through referral systems between these tiers if necessary..
